item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm okay with the labradorite photo booth, but it effects you if you see it in person or via image, I'm not down with that, it's OP and not needed.

The compulsion and mental regression stuff is also a bit lame. The core of it is, you see the thing, you become mind-wiped to worship it. Slow burn on "you become apathetic about life" then "you attack everybody in your way" is just too basic. I think it could be improved if you hammer down the conprocs and description, cut the symptomatic depression and hostility, and crank the good old "What-The-F#k"-o'meter up to 110% for what happens when you get fully mind-controlled or whatever.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of the polaroids coming out of the statue didn’t really work for me because it didn’t seem to follow any internal logic. The logs also restated the information already communicated to the reader in the description without adding anything or escalating things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting! Found this through browsing the 4000 section, and I'm quite surprised that this hasn't gotten more attention. It's short, it's sweet, and it's really solid. Only minor complaint is Footnote 2:

Roughly █% if a subject views the statue, ██% when it is viewed in person without interceding glass. A photo can be produced any time in the next few hours.

Honestly, I don't think redacting the chances of the subject being afflicted is such a good idea. It is quite important information that's kind of just left out. Other than that, excellent article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the story. I would have a minor change suggestion according to guides I read (I'm new to the site). At the Incident report you referred to a Class D person but blacked out his/her numbering, leaving only the last two letters

D-███12, after being separated…

D-███12 and reestablish…

It was suggested do not black out or hide the numbering of Disposable personnel as their numbering is the way to keep their identity a secret and further 'secrecy' is unnecessary. I think you can just replace that with random numbers and be fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Found this via the Random SCP button.

I more or less liked it, but… I can't quite explain why, can't give my vote to it yet. Maybe a re-read later will change my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is a really fantastic scip, and don't get why it isn't rated higher, good work Spooky!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Saw this on young and under 30.

+ show block
– hide block

I like the imagery with the polaroid coming out of the statue's mouth. The first half of this did a great job of setting the mood. The second half was a letdown; all that great buildup seems wasted on the ending, which reminded me of the zombie-ish monster people from stuff like 28 Days Later or Doom. I might be missing something, but it doesn't seem to fit with the setup, and I kept expecting some kind of twist or turn that never came. No vote from me, since there were still parts of the beginning I liked and the writing was solid overall.

Edit - also, welcome to the site, and even though I didn't vote on it I still want to say congratulations on what looks to be a successful first article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I've been around a long while and finally had at least a slightly original idea and wanted to give it a shot. I went through and I didn't see anything too similar so here it is. Woo.
